negro-problem-negro-and-the-law-smith.alt: Formal group portrait of the nine justices of the United States Supreme Court in 1906
negro-problem-negro-and-the-law-smith.caption: The justices of the United States Supreme Court in 1906, the federal high court before which Wilford H. Smith argued Black Americans' constitutional challenges to Southern disfranchisement and segregation statutes.
negro-problem-negro-and-the-law-smith.repository: Library of Congress, Prints and Photographs Division (LCCN 90707013; digital ID cph.3b46158)
negro-problem-negro-and-the-law-smith.license: Public domain (published in the United States before January 1, 1931)

negro-problem-talented-tenth-dubois.alt: Four well-dressed young African American women seated on the steps of a building at Atlanta University, circa 1899-1900
negro-problem-talented-tenth-dubois.caption: Four young women at Atlanta University, where Du Bois taught, photographed for his 1900 Paris Exposition exhibit; they embody the college-trained Black leadership his Talented Tenth essay calls on to uplift the race.
negro-problem-talented-tenth-dubois.repository: Library of Congress, Prints and Photographs Division (African American Photographs Assembled for 1900 Paris Exposition; LCCN 95507126)
negro-problem-talented-tenth-dubois.license: Public domain (published in the United States before January 1, 1931)
